senses.definition: plural of brewage, a beverage or drink produced by brewing, especially beer or other brewed liquids
examples: The pub offers a variety of brewages, including ales and lagers.
He sampled several brewages from the brewery.
Traditional brewages were often shared during festivals.

senses.definition: past tense and past participle of brew; to make a drink (especially beer) by mixing, boiling, and fermenting ingredients
examples: The coffee was brewed fresh this morning.
They brewed a strong batch of beer yesterday.
Her favorite tea was brewed exactly for five minutes.

senses.definition: a person or company that makes beer or other brewed drinks
examples: The brewer announced a new IPA.
Local brewers gathered for the festival.
A skilled brewer controls fermentation temperatures carefully.

senses.definition: places where beer or other brewed drinks are produced and often sold; the industry of beer making
examples: We visited several breweries on our trip.
The town is known for its breweries and pubs.
Craft breweries have grown rapidly in recent years.

senses.definition: plural of brewer; people or companies that brew beer
examples: Many brewers joined the festival this year.
The brewers compete to create the best new stout.
Small brewers contribute to local economy.

senses.definition: a rarely used or historical term referring to a brewing voyage or fleet; in modern use, some playful or archaic coinages may appear
examples: In old texts, brewership sometimes described the activity of brewing ships' provisions.
The word brewership is rarely encountered in contemporary use.

senses.definition: a place where beer is produced and often sold; a business that makes beer
examples: We toured the local brewery and saw the fermentation tanks.
The brewery opened a new tasting room.
Small breweries can be found on many streets.

senses.definition: the part of a brewery where the brewing process takes place; a building or room housing brewing equipment
examples: Steam rose from the brewhouse as the mash began.
The brewhouse layout maximizes efficiency during production.

senses.definition: plural of brewhouse; multiple brewing facilities or rooms
examples: Several brewhouses were connected to the main production line.
The new facility includes two brewhouses.

senses.definition: the act or process of making beer or other drinks by mixing, boiling, and fermenting ingredients
examples: Brewing takes patience and careful temperature control.
He is studying the science of brewing.

senses.definition: present participle of brew; to make a drink by brewing
examples: They are brewing a stout in the copper kettle.
She loves brewing coffee with a French press.

senses.definition: plural of brewing; instances or paraphernalia of the process of making drinks by brewing
examples: Ancient brewings are often found in culinary archives.
Different brewings require different temperatures.

senses.definition: obscure or archaic term; may appear as a misspelling or playful form related to 'brew' or a name
examples: Brevis is sometimes seen in old manuscripts, though not common as 'brewis'.

senses.definition: likely a misspelling or nonce formation; not a standard entry in modern English
examples: brewises is not a recognized standard term.

senses.definition: a master brewer; the head person in charge of brewing at a brewery
examples: The brewmaster approved the new recipe.
Many brewers aspire to become a brewmaster.

senses.definition: plural of brewmaster; multiple master brewers
examples: Several brewmasters spoke at the conference.
The competition included several renowned brewmasters.

senses.definition: a pub that brews its own beer on the premises
examples: We visited a cozy brewpub for dinner.
The brewpub offers a rotating list of in-house brews.

senses.definition: plural of brewpub; pubs that brew their own beer on site
examples: Several brewpubs opened in the neighborhood this year.
The brewpubs feature tasting flights.

senses.definition: drinks that are brewed, especially beer; or the act of brewing
examples: He sampled several brews at the tasting night.
This pub has many different brews on tap.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of brew; to make drinks by brewing
examples: He brews coffee every morning.
They brew ale in a small kitchen.
She brews herbal tea for guests.

senses.definition: informal slang for a bottle or can of beer; a beer itself
examples: Grab a brewski on your way home.
They cooled a few brewski after the game.

senses.definition: plural informal form of brewski; multiple beers
examples: They popped open a couple of brewskies after work.

senses.definition: alternative spelling of brewskies; plural beers
examples: They shared a couple of brewskis during the game.

senses.definition: likely a misspelling or nonstandard form; no established sense in modern English
examples: brewst is not recognized in standard dictionaries.

senses.definition: historical term for a female brewer; also a surname or name
examples: The old town had a famous Brewster family.
In old times, a brewster ran the local tavern's beer supply.

senses.definition: a mineral named after Brewster; a technical term in geology
examples: Brewsterite is a rare mineral used by collectors.

senses.definition: plural of brewster; women who brew or historically, a female brewer; or surname form
examples: Several Brewsters contributed recipes to the guild.
